description.en: Return an iterable over the named items within the package or path. The iterable returns names of resources (e.g. files) and non-resources (e.g. directories) as str. The iterable does not recurse into subdirectories. See the introduction for details on anchor and path_names. This function is roughly equivalent to: for resource in files(anchor).joinpath(*path_names).iterdir(): yield resource.name Deprecated since version 3.11: Prefer iterdir() as above, which offers more control over the resul...
